campaigns.name: PA Outreach — CFO / Director of Finance

emails.body:
**Subject: Cut your quarterly planning cycle in half**

Hi [First Name],

I work with finance leaders in Florida who are spending weeks on quarterly forecasts that could take days instead. Most are still juggling spreadsheets, historical data, and manual adjustments—leaving little room for what-if scenarios or strategy.

We just helped Idaho Forest Group cut their forecasting from 80+ hours down to 15 hours per quarter using AI-assisted planning. Their team went from firefighting data pulls to actually analyzing drivers and improving accuracy.

Would a quick 15-minute call make sense? I can show you how this works for a finance operation like yours—no pitch, just specifics on ROI and time savings.

Available this week?

Bradley Milks | IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning
bmilks11@gmail.com

**Subject: Following up—your team could cut forecasting time in half**

Hi [First Name],

Quick follow-up on the note I sent last week about quarterly planning challenges. I wanted to share something relevant: one of our financial services clients recently cut their stress testing from 4 weeks down to 2 weeks using Planning Analytics. 

For your team specifically, we're seeing CFOs reclaim about 25% of planning hours annually—time your analysts could spend on actual strategy instead of spreadsheet wrestling. Given Florida's Q4 push, this might be worth a 20-minute conversation.

Happy to walk through how this works for your forecasting cycle.

Best,

**Bradley Milks** | IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning
bmilks11@gmail.com

**Subject: Cut your forecasting cycle in half—like ICBC Argentina did**

Hi [VP Name],

We recently helped ICBC Argentina compress their bank stress tests from 4 weeks to 2 weeks by connecting real-time data feeds to AI-powered forecasting. Instead of manual spreadsheet updates, their team now runs schedule-based projections that surface patterns and seasonality automatically.

Most finance and planning teams we work with see 25% time savings department-wide—freeing analysts to focus on strategy, not data wrangling.

IBM Planning Analytics handles your historical data, real-time feeds, and market signals in one place. The AI Assistant even generates forecast summaries with confidence ranges and key drivers.

Worth 15 minutes to explore what this could look like for your operation?

Bradley Milks | IBM Account Executive, Data Trust & Planning
